title: What She Found
caption: Harriet Bloom's archive box smelled of cedar and old rain when she set it on the table at Marco's Bakery that evening. Inside, tucked between folded letters and brittle newspaper clippings, were photographs — black and white, edges soft with age — showing Willowmere as it had been in 1947. A formal ceremony. Men in hats. A banner reading 'Given in Perpetuity.' Chloe held one photograph very carefully in both hands and read the words again. In perpetuity. Not a gesture. Not sentiment. A legal phrase with weight and teeth. Detective Park leaned in from the side, voice measured, cautious: it could mean something, depending on how it was filed. And then Harriet, unhurried as always, reached into the box once more and produced a second document — folded, official-looking — and held it out to the room. The room went very quiet.
location: Marco's Bakery — evening, the long wooden table
story_notes: Harriet's photographs reveal the garden was formally donated 'in perpetuity' in 1947; Chloe recognizes this as a potential legal protection and Harriet produces a second, official-looking document.
